findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised ending anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:411 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:417 resolves into an immediate Tina/phone-lead beat on-page and does not include a forward time-jump teaser sentence; FAIL if it still jumps ahead before any consequence lands.
findings.problem: The chapter builds immediate urgency around Tina and Lyle's phone lead, then cuts to a forward teaser ('Four days later') before delivering an on-page consequence; the ending lands as setup rather than payoff.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the close at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:415, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:417 so the chapter ends on a direct consequence of Lena's move toward Tina (arrival, miss, or confrontation) rather than a time-jump hook.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Clay's revised dialogue between anchors snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:344 shows at least two distinct pressure tactics beyond repeating institutional nouns and no single institutional keyword dominates more than two lines in that sequence; FAIL if repetition remains the primary effect.
findings.problem: Clay's pressure voice over-relies on repeated institutional keywords ('command,' 'structure,' 'integrity/accountability'), which starts reading like mechanical lexical-signature reuse instead of adaptive coercion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Clay-heavy turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:303, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:337, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:344 to vary coercive tactics (procedural threat, personal leverage, silence, redirect) and reduce keyword-looping while preserving his institutional register.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised targeted spans, Lena and Clay each use contractions in at least 3 of their next 5 spoken lines when not quoting formal policy language, and at least one line per exchange shows interruption or fragment under pressure; FAIL if dialogue remains mostly full-form and ceremonially complete.
findings.problem: In high-pressure exchanges, Lena and Clay repeatedly default to polished uncontracted forms ('It is,' 'I am,' 'We are,' 'You are'), creating stiff, speechified cadence where contemporary Mississippi dialogue rules call for more contraction and fracture outside quoted policy text.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust dialogue beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:337, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:343 to keep legal precision but add contractions, interruptions, and pressure-shaped rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two 'as if' constructions remain in the revised chapter and each retained instance adds new sensory/action information not already explicit in the same sentence; FAIL if the same explanatory template still recurs as default connective tissue.
findings.problem: The repeated 'as if ...' explanatory comparison appears often enough to become a visible prose tic, flattening inference by pre-interpreting moments for the reader.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the comparison-heavy lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:113, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:405 (optionally snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:3 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:139) by replacing explanatory 'as if' tails with concrete observable detail.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:81 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:99 contains at least two explicit Lena interiority beats while preserving the 01:48/02:14/02:16/02:33/02:41 chronology; FAIL if the section remains a purely external transcript.
findings.problem: The card-reader block runs as 10 consecutive external paragraphs with no focalized internal experience, temporarily dropping close-third texture into report transcript.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:81 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:99, preserve the timestamp sequence but insert brief Lena-anchored interior beats (somatic reaction, inference pressure, memory of prior chain failures) between printout items and responses.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter_03 scene uses at most one repeated slogan noun (order/stability/contain/confidence cluster) and shows Clay applying concrete leverage moves; fail if mantra-like repetition still dominates both beats.
findings.problem: Style-bible token overfit: Clay's lexical signature (order/stability/containment nouns) becomes mantra-like in this chapter (e.g., lines 823 and 979), flattening him into repeated slogan cadence instead of adaptive coercion.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:823 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:979, keep one control-noun max, then vary the pressure language to concrete procedural threats (specific reporting restrictions, chain handling consequences, staffing moves).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
